texts.label: text
texts.orig: The usage of IEMs in electrodialysis (ED) processes is greatly contributing to various separation industries. ED has been extensively employed for brackish water desalination [1,2], organic acid recovery [3], lithium extraction from brines and the production of salts from seawater [4,5]. However, more demanding characteristics for cation exchange membranes (CEMs) in above-mentioned applications were required. For example, the selective separation of monovalent cations from multivalent cations is a highly challenging task. For instance, the production of drinking water from seawater, but the presence of multivalent cations (hardness) in seawater leads to scale formation and creating serious complications in ED [6]. Other examples can be observed in the electroplating industry for the selective separation of heavy metals cations from wastewaters [7,8]. More speci fi cally, the selective separation of Li + coexisting with Mg 2+ from brines is a challenging task for many years. texts.text: A B S T R A C T

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The advances of electrodialysis (ED) have been restricted by the trade-o ff between perm-selectivity and ion fl ux of ion exchange membranes. This article lucidly explains the fabrication of novel PVA-based hybrid membranes modi fi ed with cationic and anionic layers. Cationic layer is composed of quaternized poly (2, 6-dimethyl-1, 4phenylene oxide), while anionic layer is sulfonated poly (2, 6-dimethyl-1, 4-phenylene oxide). Interestingly, the perm-selectivity of Li + /Mg 2+ via ED was observed up to 5.16 with a feed concentration of 0.1 mol L -1 LiCl/ MgCl2 at 2.12 mA cm -2 current density. Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy was used to get further insight about the membrane and solution interfaces such as the in fl uence of a base membrane and the deposited layers, which showed the perm-selective behavior of the modi fi ed membranes. The observed high perm-selectivity of modi fi ed membranes will step forward the ED applications for production of salts and treatment of saline water comprising monovalent and divalent cations.

This article lucidly explains the fabrication of novel PVA-based hybrid membranes modi fi ed with cationic and anionic layers. Cationic layer is composed of quaternized poly (2, 6-dimethyl-1, 4phenylene oxide), while anionic layer is sulfonated poly (2, 6-dimethyl-1, 4-phenylene oxide). Interestingly, the perm-selectivity of Li + /Mg 2+ via ED was observed up to 5.16 with a feed concentration of 0.1 mol L -1 LiCl/ MgCl2 at 2.12 mA cm -2 current density. The observed high perm-selectivity of modi fi ed membranes will step forward the ED applications for production of salts and treatment of saline water comprising monovalent and divalent cations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Li + selective ionic liquid [12] and ED coupled with monovalent cation perm-selective membranes (MCPMs) have been used for selective separation of lithium from brines [6,13]. Among these methods, ED is generally recognized as ' no phase transition ' technique and is more feasible for ions separation in terms of energy consumption. However, the high cost and low separation performances of commercial MCPMs limit its application. Therefore, it is crucial to develop low cost MCPMs with high perm-selectivity. Due to the speci fi c requirement, MCPMs emerged at the right moment.

texts.orig: To advance the perm-selectivity of MCPMs, various approaches have been adopted such as chemical cross-linking [14], surface modi fi cation [15], polymer blending (or organic-inorganic hybrid) and ionic channels constructing [16,17]. texts.orig: Apart from the above-mentioned surface modi fi cation methods, recently, layer by layer (LBL) polyelectrolyte deposition method has been employed to enhance the perm-selectivity of CEMs. The LBL technique is considered the easiest approach to construct MCPMs. The polymer substrate can be modi fi ed with an alternating positively and negatively charged polymers. The modi fi ed layer's appropriate con fi guration on the membrane surface can regulate the chemistry of coating materials. For example, Nicholas et al. [21] improved the perm-selectivity of a Na fi on membrane with polyelectrolyte with increased number of coating layers up to 5.5 bilayers ((PAH/PSS)5PAH). Similarly, Abdu et al. [6] deposited a LBL assembly of (PEI/PSS)N on a Neosepta CMX cation exchange membrane. The membranes were investigated for Na + /Ca 2+ with perm-selectivity around 1.5. Cho et al. [22] reported cross-linked polyelectrolyte multilayers membranes prepared via a spray LBL technique and reported 80% divalent cation (Ca 2+ ) rejection. The reported perm-selectivity is high; however, the membrane's surface modi fi cation required several coating layers to increase the perm-selectivity.

The modi fi ed layer's appropriate con fi guration on the membrane surface can regulate the chemistry of coating materials. For example, Nicholas et al. [21] improved the perm-selectivity of a Na fi on membrane with polyelectrolyte with increased number of coating layers up to 5.5 bilayers ((PAH/PSS)5PAH). Similarly, Abdu et al. [6] deposited a LBL assembly of (PEI/PSS)N on a Neosepta CMX cation exchange membrane. The membranes were investigated for Na + /Ca 2+ with perm-selectivity around 1.5. Cho et al. [22] reported cross-linked polyelectrolyte multilayers membranes prepared via a spray LBL technique and reported 80% divalent cation (Ca 2+ ) rejection. The reported perm-selectivity is high; however, the membrane's surface modi fi cation required several coating layers to increase the perm-selectivity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Inspired from the LBL technique, in this article, we prepared a novel PVA-based hybrid membrane as a base membrane and then modi fi ed with quaternized poly (2, 6-dimethyl-1, 4-phenylene oxide) (QPPO) and sulfonated poly (2, 6-dimethyl-1, 4-phenylene oxide) (SPPO) by spray coating to increase the perm-selectivity for Li + /Mg 2+ system. The electrostatic interaction between oppositely charge polymers is the driving force to construct MCPMs. The high perm-selectivity of the prepared membranes can be attributed to the anionic and cationic chemistry of deposited layers to make the membrane with desired characteristics [23]. Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy was used to explore the interaction between surface layers and an electrolyte, particularly, the resistances created due to the formation of di ff usion boundary layer (DBL) and the double-layer (DL) [24]. Moreover, FTIR and SEM were used to study the chemical compositions and morphology of the membranes.

texts.orig: A 20 mL solution of PVA (1 g in 19 mL of DMSO at 103 °C for 2.5 h) was prepared. Then, an amount to 0.2 g of DTPA was added and stirred for 2 h. The temperature was decreased to 60 °C and then TEOS (0.3 g) along with 1 mL of 0.01 mol L -1 HCl were added and the mixture was stirred at 60 °C for 24 h to ensure the sol-gel process. The reaction mixture was cast over the glass plate at 60 °C for 24 h. The dried membrane was peeled o ff and thermally crosslinked as reported in our previous work [25]. The membrane was designated as P-COOH. The reaction is summarized in the Scheme 1.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: QPPO was prepared by functionalization of BPPO with a tertiary amine via Menshutkin reaction [26]. A calculated amount of MDEA (2.85 mmol) was added to 10 mL BPPO solution (1 g in 9 mL NMP). The reaction was carried out for 18 h at 40 °C as in Scheme 2. The reaction mixture was precipitated in ethyl acetate and washed several times with diethyl ether. The prepared QPPO polymer was dried at 35 °C for 48 h. The polymer was con fi rmed by 1 H NMR ( DMSO -d6 , 400 MHz) (Fig. S1 ESI † ).

The reaction mixture was precipitated in ethyl acetate and washed several times with diethyl ether. The prepared QPPO polymer was dried at 35 °C for 48 h. The polymer was con fi rmed by 1 H NMR ( DMSO -d6 , 400 MHz) (Fig. S1 ESI † ).

It comprises four compartments. 0.3 mol L -1 Na2SO4 and 0.5 mol L -1 NaCl solutions were used to circulate in outer and central compartments, respectively. The membrane under examination (with an e ff ective area of 7.07 cm 2 ) in central compartments was separated from outer compartments via two auxiliary membranes (Na fi on). The current was increased gradually using a potentiostat/galvanostat (WYL1703, Hangzhou Siling Electrical Instrument Ltd.) between the two working electrodes made-up of titanium coated with ruthenium. The limiting current density was calculated from the I e V curves.

texts.orig: 2.6. Electrodialysis
texts.text: 2.6. Electrodialysis

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A conventional procedure was adopted by employing a modi fi ed CEM as reported [27]. The ED setup was classi fi ed into three compartments and designated as i.e. electrolyte compartment (cathode and anode), concentrated and diluted compartments as portrayed in Fig. 2. Anode and cathode electrodes were made-up of titanium coated with ruthenium. A modi fi ed CEM surface facing the diluted compartment with an area of 7.07 cm 2 was held between diluted and concentrated compartments. A potentiostat-galvanostat (WYL1703, Hangzhou Siling Electrical Instrument Ltd.) instrument was applied to produce a constant current of 15.0 mA. A solution of 0.3 mol L -1 Na2SO4 was used as an electrolyte solution. The diluted compartment was fi lled with 100 mL of 0.1 mol L -1 LiCl/MgCl2 solution, while 100 mL solution of 0.1 mol L -1 KCl was used in the concentrated compartment. The dissolved cations such as Li + , Mg 2+ travel towards the cathode via CEM, while Cl -ions move towards the anode via AEMs to ful fi l the principle of electroneutrality. texts.orig: DTPA was introduced in the PVA backbone via a sol-gel-process. The membrane was then crosslinked by heat treatment and modi fi ed with QPPO and SPPO polymers. The base membrane and the coating layers in comparison to QPPO and SPPO were con fi rmed by ATR-FTIR as in Fig. 4. The bands appeared at 1739 cm -1 and 1621 cm -1 can be related to the stretching vibration of (C=O) from ester groups and C e N in the base membrane [31,32]. Moreover, the band around 1092 cm -1 showed the Si-O-Si and Si-O-C networks and approved the crosslinked nature of membrane [33]. After modi fi cation with QPPO polymer, some new bands were appeared such as 1634 cm -1 1464 cm -1 and 1025 cm -1 , which con fi rmed the stretching vibrations C e C and C e O of benzene rings and the presence -N + (CH3)3 in QPPO layer [34 -36]. Further coating of P-COOH-Q membrane with SPPO layer, the band appeared at 1188 cm -1 and 1066 cm -1 showed the existence of -SO3 -group in the SPPO layer of the modi fi ed membrane and pristine SPPO [37]. texts.orig: The membranes thermal stability was investigated at 10 °C min -1 in N2 environment. As given in Fig. 5, three main degradation stages were observed. The fi rst weight loss observed before 149 °C is attributed to the evaporation of absorbed water [38]. The second weight loss was observed in the range of 155 -300 °C and can be ascribed to the decarboxylation [39], breaking of -N + (CH3)3 and -SO 3 -groups along with the decomposition ether functional group of SPPO and QPPO polymer backbones [40].

The decreased in thermal stability i.e. P-COOH >P-COOH-Q>P-COOH-QSQ of membranes can be related to QPPO and SPPO layers. The presence of QPPO layer on the P-COOH-Q membrane made the membrane relatively less stable due to the presence of cationic head groups, which usually lowers thermal stability [30,41]. For reference, we also prepared free standing QPPO-GA membrane which showed lower thermal stability. The lower thermal stability of P-COOH-QSQ can be associated to the presence of -SO3 -(SPPO degraded more rapidly) and -N + (CH3)3 groups as observed in the pristine SPPO and QPPO-GA [30,42]. The third weight loss originated at temperature > 400 °C and could be attributed to the decomposition of whole polymeric backbone.

texts.orig: 2.10. Morphology
texts.text: 2.10. Morphology

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The morphology of prepared membranes was analyzed by SEM. Surfaces (a, c, and e) and cross-sections (b, d, and f) of P-COOH, PCOOH-Q and P-COOH-QSQ membranes are given in Fig. 6. The apparent homogenous morphology of the base membrane is attributed to the compatibility between the -COOH groups of the loading moiety and PVA. The surface analysis showed that the P-COOH-Q is relatively smooth and homogeneous in comparison to P-COOH membrane. The uniformity can be associated to the crosslinking of QPPO layer with the base membrane via GA [43]. Further coating with QS layers made the PCOOH-QSQ membrane slightly rough in appearances with small white spots.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The cross-sectional analysis of P-COOH-Q revealed a dense uniform layer of QPPO with thicknesses of about 9.13 μ m. The membrane was further coated with SQ layers. The adherent sandwich structure of QSQ to the base membrane is accredited to the presence of electrostatic and π -π interactions arising from the benzene rings of SPPO and QPPO polymers [8,44]. From the cross-section of P-COOH-QSQ membrane, a dense layer of 26.1 μ m can be clearly di ff erentiated from the P-COOH layer. These layers are fi rmly integrated with PVA-based hybrid membrane. The Q and SQ layers with a base membrane retained a dense morphology. Keeping in view the SEM results, we may conclude that LBL technique can be employed to fabricate a PVA-based monovalent cation perm-selective membrane with uniform morphology.

texts.orig: EIS is an e ff ective technique to study the active resistances confronted by cations permeating through the membrane due to the formation of boundary layers in an electrochemical cell [24,45 -48]. Fig. 7 shows the Nyquist plots, which consist of Zr and Zi along x-axis and frequency ( ω ) along y-axis. The ( ω ) is a hidden parameter and its value decrease from left to right on the x-axis. Fig. 7a represents two overlapped semi-circles related to the electric double layers (EDLs) and di ff usion boundary layers (DBLs). These layers were formed due to the fl ow of electric current, beside the surface of a base membrane and inside the electrolyte solution, respectively. Whereas, the inclined line at low frequency is an indication of semi-in fi nite linear di ff usion behavior through the base membrane [47,49,50]. In Fig. 7b, two additional semi-circles at medium and low frequencies showed the contribution of EDLs and DBLs at the modi fi ed surface of the membrane. A similar but increased number of semi-circles were observed in Fig. 7c, which clearly indicated the presence of additional modi fi ed layers and were con fi rmed in cross-sectional SEM micrographs in Fig. 6. In contrast to Fig. 7a, the semi-circle for P-COOH-Q and P-COOH-QSQ membranes at low frequency indicated restricted di ff usion due to modi fi ed layers at the surface of the base membrane. texts.orig: 2.12. Current-voltage curve (I e V)
texts.text: 2.12. Current-voltage curve (I e V)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Generally, the I e V curves are employed to investigate the transport of ions near and inside IEMs [52]. The I e V curve can be classi fi ed into three regions. The fi rst is ' ohmic region ' appears at low current densities and signi fi es a linear relation between applied current and the voltage drop. The second is ' plateau region ' which de fi nes the limiting current density iLim . The third region of the I e V curve ' over limiting region ' is considered the advanced stage related to concentration polarization.

The second is ' plateau region ' which de fi nes the limiting current density iLim . texts.label: text
texts.orig: The I e V curves of prepared membranes are given in Fig. 8. The variation in slope declares the increase of electrical resistance brought by the modi fi cation. The cationic layer at the surface of the membrane resists the transport of cations across the membrane particularly divalent cations. Furthermore, the total coverage of the base membrane with QSQ layers has some bene fi ts to produce dense and defect-free surface layers. The second part of the curve is ' plateau region ' in Fig. 8, the length of this region extended and the over-limiting current was delayed to a great degree and is attributed to the surface homogeneity due to the coating of QPPO and SPPO layers and these observations are identical to the work reported elsewhere [53]. However, some confl icting observations have also been reported that the surface modi fi cations decrease the iLim and reduce the length of the plateau region [54]. Therefore, it is hard to establish well that the surface modi fi cation would amplify the iLim and which is beyond the scope of this work.

texts.orig: cationic layer. However, the Mg 2+ fl ux was greatly decreased to 1.3 × 10 -9 mol cm -2 s -1 and improved the perm-selectivity from 1.46 to 3.83. The increase perm-selectivity could be linked to the construction of cationic layer, which provides greater repulsion for the divalent cations. When investigated P-COOH-QS membrane, the Li + fl ux was increased to 11 × 10 -9 mol cm -2 s -1 while the Mg 2+ fl ux was less increased as compared to P-COOH. The observed perm-selectivity was around 1.74. The decreased in perm-selectivity of P-COOH-QS in comparison to P-COOH-Q membrane could be linked to the SPPO layer. The hydrophilic SPPO layer is highly permeable to cations due to strong electrostatic attraction else counterbalance the cationic head groups of QPPO layer. The less prominent e ff ect of QPPO layer and high permeability to cations increase the cations fl uxes, and hence less permselective. The interception of sulfonic group within the layers and the decrease of perm-selectivity with increase of ionic fl ux was expected as reported by Abdu et al. [6]. However, the perm-selectivity is high as compared to P-COOH. Further coating to P-COOH-QSQ, the Li + fl ux was recorded to 4.47 × 10 -9 mol cm -2 s -1 , but the substantial decrease in Mg 2+ fl ux i.e. 0.86 × 10 -9 mol cm -2 s -1 led to high permselectivity 5.16. The observed results authenticate the preparation strategy for monovalent cation selective membranes. Furthermore, the EIS results from Fig. 7, showed that the net charge on membrane surface a ff ects the distribution of ions at the solution/membrane interface and develop electric double layers (EDLs). The EDLs comprise of a Stern layer (SL) and di ff use layer (DL), the former produced by the strong electrostatic interactions between membrane surface and counter-ions while the later established with loosely bond ions. These layers strictly control the perm-selective behavior of membranes. The observed permselectivity was high as compared to a modi fi ed Neosepta CMX membrane for Na + /Ca 2+ perm-selectivity i.e. 1.4 [6], a nano fi ltration (NF) membrane for Li + /Mg 2+ perm-selectivity i.e. 3.5 [55] and a nano- fi brous composite membrane for Na + /Mg 2+ system with perm-selectivity of 1.62 [30].

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Conclusion
texts.text: 3. Conclusion

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We prepared novel PVA-based hybrid membranes modi fi ed with QPPO and SPPO layers, hence producing a chemically stable heterojunction. The electrostatic and crosslinking gluing in LBL scheme created well designed layers on surface of the base membrane. The base and modi fi ed membranes were investigated for cations fractionation using feed concentration of 0.1 mol L -1 LiCl/MgCl2 via ED. The fabricated selective layers unveiled good perm-selectivity up to 5.16. The high perm-selectivity could be attributed to the integration of cationic head groups over the base membrane. The EIS and I e V analysis further revealed the membrane surface characteristics for better understanding of the improved perm-selectivity. The prepared membranes are thermally stable and could be employed at high temperature. It can be concluded that the surface modi fi cation procedure such as LBL coating is economical, environment friendly and easy to modify a polymer substrate to induce new capabilities in membrane materials for numerous industrial applications.
texts.text: We prepared novel PVA-based hybrid membranes modi fi ed with QPPO and SPPO layers, hence producing a chemically stable heterojunction. The electrostatic and crosslinking gluing in LBL scheme created well designed layers on surface of the base membrane. The base and modi fi ed membranes were investigated for cations fractionation using feed concentration of 0.1 mol L -1 LiCl/MgCl2 via ED. The fabricated selective layers unveiled good perm-selectivity up to 5.16. The high perm-selectivity could be attributed to the integration of cationic head groups over the base membrane. The EIS and I e V analysis further revealed the membrane surface characteristics for better understanding of the improved perm-selectivity. The prepared membranes are thermally stable and could be employed at high temperature. It can be concluded that the surface modi fi cation procedure such as LBL coating is economical, environment friendly and easy to modify a polymer substrate to induce new capabilities in membrane materials for numerous industrial applications.
